title: Sudan Crude Oil Exports via Bashayer
section_path: Sudan Crude Oil Exports via Bashayer
section_path_text: Sudan Crude Oil Exports via Bashayer
summary:
Main
Snapshot of Crude and Product Freight Rates, Supply-Demand
Week 27, 4 July, 2025
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-07-04 124144.png | assets/2025-07-04_sudan-crude-oil-exports-via-bashayer_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_c1a18bfc9d40.png
Since late April 2023, Sudan has been gripped by an internal conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), which has significantly disrupted crude oil exports via the Bashayer erminal. Although most of the crude, particularly Dar Blend and Nile Blend, is produced in South Sudan, it must be exported through Sudan's Red Sea port infrastructure. The civil war has led to operational uncertainty, suspected pipeline disruptions, and a sharp reduction in export volumes, which dropped to just 2 million barrels in April.

Although most of the crude, particularly Dar Blend and Nile Blend, is produced in South Sudan, it must be exported through Sudan's Red Sea port infrastructure.
